title: Special Registers Deep Dive
subtitle: {key:%}, {key:#}, {key::}, {key:.}, {key:/}, {key:=}, {key:_} — the read-only and computed ones.
summary: Beyond the named and numbered registers, Vim has a set of special registers that hold computed or context-dependent values: filename (%), alternate file (#), last command (:), last insert (.), last search (/), expression (=), and the black hole (_).

blocks.text: Beyond {key:a}–{key:z} and {key:0}–{key:9}, Vim has a handful of *special* registers that hold computed values. Most are read-only.

rows: {key:"}{key:.}{key:p} to paste what you just typed
{key:"}{key:_}{key:d}{key:d} delete without affecting any other register

blocks.title: The Black Hole Register
blocks.text:
{key:"}{key:_} is the *delete-without-yanking* register. Writes go in; reads return nothing.

  • {key:"}{key:_}{key:d}{key:d} deletes a line *without* clobbering the unnamed register or rotating the numbered registers.
  • {key:"}{key:_}{key:d}{key:i}{key:w} deletes a word; your previous yank is preserved for {key:p}.

Indispensable when refactoring: yank some text, then delete other text without losing your yank.

blocks.title: The Expression Register
blocks.text:
{key:"}{key:=} opens a prompt for any Vim expression. The result is stored as the register contents.

  • {key:"}{key:=}{key:s}{key:t}{key:r}{key:f}{key:t}{key:i}{key:m}{key:e}{key:(}{key:'}{key:%}{key:c}{key:'}{key:)}{key:Enter}{key:p} → pastes the current date.
  • {key:"}{key:=}{key:l}{key:i}{key:n}{key:e}{key:(}{key:'}{key:.}{key:'}{key:)}{key:Enter}{key:p} → pastes the current line number.
  • {key:"}{key:=}{key:5}{key:*}{key:7}{key:Enter}{key:p} → pastes 35.

From *insert mode*, {key:Ctrl-R}{key:=} does the same. From the command line, {key:Ctrl-R}{key:=} too.
